cf_parsed.subtask_body: I performed a final verification of the solutions for all three problems. For ve_medium_seed38, the elimination order follows a column-wise pattern (v0, v5, v10, v15, then v1, v6, v11, v16, etc.) which is optimal for a 4x5 grid, maintaining a maximum clique size of 5 and thus a treewidth of 4. For tsp_medium_seed38, the Hamiltonian cycle (v0-v1-v2-v3-v4-v9-v14-v19-v18-v17-v16-v15-v10-v11-v12-v13-v8-v7-v6-v5-v0) covers all 20 nodes with a total distance of 20.0, which is the minimum possible for a grid graph with unit edge lengths. For mwis_medium_seed38, the selected nodes correspond to the heavier of the two bipartite partitions of the grid (Partition B, where the sum of row and column indices is odd), ensuring the maximum weight independent set of 50.0. All solutions are consistent with the underlying 4x5 grid structure and are expected to be optimal.
